message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Emerging severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus type 2 (SARS-CoV-2) variants, especially the Omicron variant, have impaired the efficacy of existing vaccines and most therapeutic antibodies, highlighting the need for additional antibody-based tools that can efficiently neutralize emerging SARS-CoV-2 variants. The use of a “single” agent to simultaneously target multiple distinct epitopes on the spike is desirable to overcome the neutralizing escape of SARS-CoV-2 variants. Herein, we generated a human-derived IgG-like bispecific antibody (bsAb), Bi-Nab
                  <jats:sub>35B5-47D10</jats:sub>
                  , which successfully retained the specificity and simultaneously bound to the two distinct epitopes on RBD and S2. Bi-Nab
                  <jats:sub>35B5-47D10</jats:sub>
                  showed improved spike binding breadth among wild-type (WT) SARS-CoV-2, variants of concern (VOCs) and variants being monitored (VBMs) compared with its parental mAbs. Furthermore, pseudotyped virus neutralization demonstrated that Bi-Nab
                  <jats:sub>35B5-47D10</jats:sub>
                  can efficiently neutralize VBMs including Alpha (B.1.1.7), Beta (B.1.351) and Kappa (B.1.617.1) and VOCs including Delta (B.1.617.2), Omicron BA.1 and Omicron BA.2. Crucially, Bi-Nab
                  <jats:sub>35B5-47D10</jats:sub>
                  substantially improved neutralizing activity against Omicron BA.1 (IC
                  <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                  = 27.3 ng/mL) and Omicron BA.2 (IC
                  <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                  = 121.1 ng/mL) compared with their parental mAbs. Therefore, Bi-Nab
                  <jats:sub>35B5-47D10</jats:sub>
                  represents a potential effective countermeasure against SARS-CoV-2 Omicron and other variants of concern.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Importance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The new highly contagious SARS-CoV-2 Omicron variant caused substantial breakthrough infections and has become the dominant strain in countries across the world. Omicron variants usually bear high mutations in the spike protein and exhibit considerable escape of most potent neutralization monoclonal antibodies and reduced efficacy of current COVID-19 vaccines. The development of neutralizing antibodies with potent efficacy against the Omicron variant is still an urgent priority. Here, we generated a bsAb, Bi-Nab
                    <jats:sub>35B5-47D10,</jats:sub>
                    that simultaneously targets SARS-CoV-2 RBD and S2 and improved neutralizing potency and breadth against SARS-CoV-2 WT and the tested variants compared with their parental antibodies. Notably, Bi-Nab
                    <jats:sub>35B5-47D10</jats:sub>
                    has more potent neutralizing activity against the VOC Omicron pseudotyped virus. Therefore, Bi-Nab
                    <jats:sub>35B5-47D10</jats:sub>
                    is a feasible and potentially effective strategy to treat and prevent COVID-19.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: A Bispecific Antibody Targeting RBD and S2 Potently Neutralizes SARS-CoV-2 Omicron and Other Variants of Concern
